batches.title: Quinoa Base, Grilled Chicken, Roasted Mediterranean Vegetables (Monday Lunch)
steps: Rinse 200g quinoa in cold water. Cook in 400 ml salted water for 15 min. Fluff with fork and transfer to a shallow tray to cool completely.
Season 3 chicken breasts (about 600g total) with salt, pepper, dried oregano, and lemon zest. Set aside.
Dice 2 medium courgettes, 1 red bell pepper, cherry tomatoes (400g total). Toss with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper, dried oregano in a large roasting tin.
Roast vegetables at 200°C for 20 min until just tender, stirring halfway. Remove and cool on a tray.
Heat a non-stick pan or grill over medium-high heat. Sear chicken breasts 6–7 min per side until golden and cooked through. Transfer to a cutting board and cool for 5 min, then slice.
Once all components are cool, portion into 2 containers: 100g/70g quinoa base, 180g/140g sliced chicken, 250g/200g roasted veg per person.
yields: 600 g grilled and sliced chicken breast (serves 2)

day_of_assembly.Monday: Lunch — Mediterranean Grilled Chicken with Roasted Vegetables & Quinoa: reheat container 2 min, top with fresh lemon juice and squeeze over the plate.

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Portioned Greek Yogurt & Walnuts (Mon/Thu/Sat PM Snack)
steps: Remove 1.2 kg Greek yogurt from fridge.
Measure and portion 200g yogurt into one container and 150g into another (Diego/Diana).
Repeat to fill 3 sets of containers (Monday, Thursday, Saturday).
Portion walnuts: 25g (Diego) and 20g (Diana) into 3 small zip-lock bags or containers.

day_of_assembly.Monday: PM Snack — Greek Yogurt with Walnuts & Berries: transfer yogurt to a serving bowl, top with walnuts and fresh berries.
day_of_assembly.Thursday: PM Snack — Greek Yogurt with Walnuts & Berries: transfer yogurt to a serving bowl, top with walnuts and fresh berries.
day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (dairy is only safe ~5 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.container: sealed original packaging or airtight container
storage.safety_note: Check use-by date on packaging. Opened yogurt: 5–7 days.

batches.title: Baked White Fish, Roasted Green Beans & Sweet Potato (Monday Dinner)
steps: Trim 500 g green beans. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper. Spread on a roasting tray.
Roast at 200°C for 12 min until tender-crisp. Cool on the tray.
Dice 400 g sweet potato into 1 cm cubes. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper. Spread on a separate roasting tray.
Roast at 200°C for 18 min until golden and tender. Cool on the tray.
Pat 4 fish fillets (about 640g total: 180g × 2 + 140g × 2, or similar mix) dry with paper towels.
Season each fillet with salt, pepper, dried dill, and thin lemon slices on top. Place on foil-lined tray.
Bake at 200°C for 10 min until opaque and flakes easily. Cool slightly.
Portion into containers: one set per person with 180g/140g fish, 180g/140g green beans, 150g/110g sweet potato.

day_of_assembly.Monday: Dinner — Baked White Fish with Lemon, Green Beans & Sweet Potato: reheat in a 180°C oven 5 min or microwave 2 min, squeeze fresh lemon over the top and serve.

storage.container: airtight container on ice or coldest shelf
storage.safety_note: Fish spoils fast. Use within 2 days; 1 day is safer. Reheat to 74 °C.

batches.title: Asian Chicken Stir-Fry with Bok Choy & Brown Rice (Tuesday Lunch)
steps: Measure 200 g brown rice. Rinse and cook in 400 ml salted water for 45 min (or follow package). Fluff and cool on a baking tray.
Slice 600 g chicken breast into 1 cm wide strips. Season with salt and white pepper. Set aside.
Wash 1 kg fresh bok choy and cut into quarters lengthwise. Bring a pot of salted water to boil, blanch bok choy for 2 min, then drain and cool.
Toast 2 tbsp sesame seeds in a dry pan over medium heat for 1–2 min, stirring constantly, until fragrant. Pour into a small container and cool.
Once rice is cool, portion 100 g (Diego) and 70 g (Diana) into containers. Repeat portions for second meal if needed.
Portion sliced raw chicken (180 g Diego / 140 g Diana), blanched bok choy (200 g / 160 g), and sesame seeds (12 g / 10 g) into separate containers or bags to keep fresh.

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Lunch — Asian Chicken Stir-Fry with Bok Choy, Sesame & Brown Rice: heat 1 tsp sesame oil + 1 tbsp soy sauce in a small pan, add chicken strips and bok choy, warm for 2 min, stir into rice base, sprinkle sesame seeds.

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Pan-Seared Salmon, Roasted Asparagus & Boiled New Potatoes (Tuesday Dinner)
steps: Boil 250 g new potatoes (halved or whole, skin on) in salted water for 15 min until just tender. Drain and cool.
Trim 400 g fresh asparagus. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper. Spread on a roasting tray.
Roast asparagus at 200°C for 10 min until tender. Cool on the tray.
Pat 4 salmon fillets (about 640g total: 180g × 2 + 140g × 2 mix) completely dry with paper towels.
Season with salt, pepper, fresh dill. Heat a non-stick pan over medium-high heat.
Place salmon skin-side down in the pan. Cook 4 min without moving, then flip and cook 3 min more. Transfer to a plate and cool slightly.
Once cool, portion: 180g/140g salmon, 180g/140g roasted asparagus, 150g/110g boiled potatoes per person into containers.

day_of_assembly.Tuesday: Dinner — Pan-Seared Salmon with Roasted Asparagus & New Potatoes: reheat in a 180°C oven 4 min or microwave gently 2 min, drizzle with fresh lemon juice and serve.

storage.container: airtight container on ice or coldest shelf
storage.safety_note: Fish spoils fast. Use within 2 days; 1 day is safer. Reheat to 74 °C.

batches.title: Soft-Boiled Eggs (Wed, Sat Breakfast)
steps: Fill a pot with enough cold water to cover 7 eggs by about 5 cm.
Bring water to a rolling boil over high heat.
Carefully lower 7 free-range eggs into the boiling water using a spoon.
Set a timer for 6 min (soft yolk, set white).
After 6 min, transfer eggs to a bowl of ice-cold water. Let cool for at least 5 min.
Drain and store in a closed container in the fridge. They will keep for 4 days.

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Breakfast — Boiled Egg with Sourdough, Cheese & Raspberries: toast sourdough, remove one egg from fridge, halve into egg cup, arrange toasted bread, cheddar cheese, and fresh raspberries on the side.
day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (eggs cooked is only safe ~4 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.safety_note: Hard-boiled eggs: 1 week in shell, 5 days peeled in water. Scrambled/cooked dishes: 3–4 days.

batches.title: Mexican-Style Chicken Lettuce Wraps with Black Beans (Wednesday Lunch)
steps: Cook 200 g dried black beans in 600 ml water for 45 min until tender (or use 2 cans drained, about 500g cooked). Drain if necessary.
Season cooked beans with 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp chilli powder, salt, pepper. Cool.
Dice 1 red bell pepper. Set aside.
Season 600 g chicken breast with salt, pepper, cumin, chilli powder. Grill or pan-sear 6–7 min per side. Cool and shred or dice into bite-sized pieces.
Toss warm shredded chicken with juice of 1 lime and a pinch more cumin. Cool.
Wash and separate butterhead lettuce leaves (discard damaged outer leaves). Set aside in a damp cloth to keep crisp.
Portion into containers: black beans (80 g / 60 g), shredded chicken (180 g / 140 g), diced pepper (100 g / 80 g), lettuce leaves (150 g / 120 g), lime wedge per meal.
yields: 600 g shredded chicken breast with lime

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Lunch — Mexican-Style Chicken Lettuce Wraps with Black Beans & Lime: lay out 2–3 lettuce leaves, fill each with 1 tbsp black beans, chicken, diced pepper, squeeze fresh lime over wraps.

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Turkey Meatballs in Tomato Sauce, Sautéed Courgette & Pasta (Wednesday Dinner)
steps: Cook 150 g whole-wheat pasta until al dente. Drain, cool, portion into containers (100 g / 70 g per person).
Mix 400 g ground turkey with 2 tbsp wholemeal breadcrumbs, 1 large egg, salt, pepper, dried oregano. Form into 12 equal meatballs.
Place meatballs on a baking tray lined with parchment paper. Bake at 200°C for 12 min until cooked through.
Heat 300 ml passata (canned tomato sauce) in a pot. Add baked meatballs. Simmer gently for 5 min. Cool completely.
Dice 600 g fresh courgette (skin on). Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Sauté courgette with salt and pepper for 5–7 min until tender and lightly golden. Cool.
Portion into containers: cooked pasta (100 g / 70 g), 3 meatballs + 100 g tomato sauce (150 g / 110 g total), sautéed courgette (150 g / 120 g), parmesan (10 g / 8 g) in a small bag.

day_of_assembly.Wednesday: Dinner — Turkey Meatballs with Tomato Sauce, Courgette & Pasta: reheat container in a microwave 2 min or small pan 3 min until warm, top with fresh basil and light parmesan.

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Grilled Beef Sirloin, Roasted Root Vegetables & Red Lentils (Thursday Lunch)
steps: Cook 150 g red lentils in 450 ml water for 20 min until tender. Fluff and cool. Portion into containers (80 g / 60 g per person).
Peel and dice beetroot, parsnips, carrots (400 g total): roughly 1 cm cubes. Toss with 2 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper, dried thyme.
Spread on a roasting tray. Roast at 200°C for 25 min, stirring halfway, until golden and tender. Cool.
Season 3 beef sirloin steaks (about 600g total) with salt and pepper on both sides.
Heat a cast-iron or heavy pan over medium-high heat until very hot. Sear steaks 4–5 min per side for medium-rare. Transfer to a cutting board and cool, then slice.
Portion into containers: lentil base (80 g / 60 g), roasted veg (240 g / 190 g), sliced beef (180 g / 140 g) per person.
yields: 600 g grilled and sliced beef sirloin

day_of_assembly.Thursday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (red meat is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 4).

storage.safety_note: Store in coldest part of fridge. Reheat to 74 °C before serving.

batches.title: Baked Cod, Roasted Mixed Root Vegetables (Thursday Dinner)
steps: Dice carrot, celery, sweet potato (300 g total): roughly 1 cm cubes. Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper.
Spread on a roasting tray. Roast at 200°C for 20 min until tender. Cool.
Pat 4 cod fillets (about 640g total: 180g × 2 + 140g × 2 mix) dry with paper towels.
Season each fillet with salt, pepper, fresh dill. Place on foil-lined tray with thin lemon slices on top.
Bake at 200°C for 10 min until opaque and flakes easily. Cool slightly.
Portion into containers: 180g/140g cod, 200g/160g roasted veg per person.
yields: 300 g roasted carrot, celery, sweet potato mix

day_of_assembly.Thursday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (fish seafood is only safe ~2 day(s); this is day 4).

storage.container: airtight container on ice or coldest shelf
storage.safety_note: Fish spoils fast. Use within 2 days; 1 day is safer. Reheat to 74 °C.

batches.title: Baked Chicken with Roasted Vegetables & Whole Couscous (Friday Lunch)
steps: Cook 150 g whole-grain couscous in 300 ml boiling vegetable broth. Cover, remove from heat, let stand 5 min. Fluff and cool.
Dice 600 g chicken breast. Season with salt, pepper, paprika. Spread on a roasting tray.
Roast at 200°C for 15 min until golden and cooked through. Cool.
Dice 1 courgette and halve cherry tomatoes (250 g total). Toss with 1 tbsp olive oil, salt, pepper.
Roast at 200°C for 15 min. Cool.
Prepare or use 100 g shop-bought hummus.
Portion into containers: couscous (100 g / 70 g), diced chicken (180 g / 140 g), roasted veg (200 g / 160 g), hummus (30 g / 20 g separately), fresh parsley (½ tbsp per meal in a small bag).
yields: 250 g roasted courgette and cherry tomatoes

day_of_assembly.Friday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (poultry is only safe ~3 day(s); this is day 5).

storage.safety_note: Cool quickly; never leave poultry at room temp > 2 h. Reheat to 74 °C through.

batches.title: Chickpea & Vegetable Tagine with Whole-Wheat Couscous (Saturday Lunch)
steps: Cook 300 g dried chickpeas in 800 ml water for 50 min until very tender (or drain 2 cans, about 500 g cooked total). Drain.
Dice carrot, courgette, red bell pepper (300 g total). Heat a large pot over medium heat.
Sauté diced vegetables briefly (2 min) with 1 tbsp olive oil to soften slightly.
Add cooked chickpeas, 400 ml vegetable broth, 1 tsp cumin, ½ tsp ground cinnamon, 1 tsp paprika, salt, pepper. Simmer for 10 min.
Add 50 g chopped dried apricots. Simmer 5 min more. Cool completely.
Cook 150 g whole-wheat couscous in 300 ml vegetable broth. Let stand 5 min, fluff, cool.
Portion into containers: couscous (100 g / 70 g), tagine (250 g / 200 g), dried apricots visible, fresh cilantro (½ tbsp per meal in a small bag).
yields: 500 g chickpea and vegetable tagine with dried apricots

day_of_assembly.Saturday: ⚠ Cook fresh today — do NOT use the Sunday batch (legumes is only safe ~5 day(s); this is day 6).

storage.safety_note: Cooked beans and lentils keep well. Freeze in portions for quick meals.
